description_long: This object is a vintage folding camera with a bellows design, featuring a prominent circular lens assembly at the front and a rectangular, boxy body. The camera's exterior is primarily brown with metallic accents, including visible rivets and reinforced corners, giving it a robust, industrial appearance. The bellows, made of a dark, accordion-like material, extend from the main body to the lens, supported by metal struts on either side. The top of the camera has a flat surface, possibly for a viewfinder or film loading mechanism, and the sides display additional metallic plates and fasteners. The back of the camera features a large metal plate with engraved markings, suggesting a model or manufacturer label. Overall, the camera exudes a classic, early 20th-century aesthetic, suitable for display or as a collectible item.
description: A vintage folding camera with a brown boxy body, metallic accents, and a prominent bellows and lens assembly. The design features industrial details and a classic early 20th-century look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular lens assembly, extended bellows, and metallic struts supporting the lens.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the metal struts, side plates, and the accordion-like bellows structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a large metal plate with engraved markings and reinforced corners.
description_view_3: Right side view showing additional metallic details, fasteners, and the side profile of the bellows and lens.
